forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8880'
+title: Investigate flaky ConnectionTests.aRequestTimeoutThatPropagatesUncaughtStillClosesTheConnectionAndReapsTheProcess
+---
+## What
+`Tests/FoundationModelsCodeContextTests/ConnectionTests.swift:317`'s test `aRequestTimeoutThatPropagatesUncaughtStillClosesTheConnectionAndReapsTheProcess()` intermittently fails with:
+
+```
+Expectation failed: kill(capturedPID, 0) == -1 && errno == ESRCH
+```
+
+Observed failing once in 4 consecutive `swift test` runs (2026-07-15), passing the other 3 times. Discovered incidentally while verifying task 01KXK6WVQ6584D77AK33NFTMJZ (unrelated Examples/ManagerExample addition) — this test file was not touched by that change.
+
+The assertion checks that a killed/reaped subprocess's PID no longer exists (`kill(pid, 0)` returns -1 with `ESRCH`). This is inherently a race against the OS actually finishing reaping the process; under load or scheduler jitter the check can run before the process is fully gone.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Root-cause why the reap-completion check can observe the PID as still present
+- [ ] Either add a bounded retry/poll (e.g. poll `kill(pid, 0)` for a short timeout instead of asserting once immediately after the close call) or otherwise make the assertion deterministic
+- [ ] Loop `swift test --filter ConnectionTests` at least 20x locally with no failures before closing
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `swift test --filter ConnectionTests` passes reliably across repeated runs
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,20 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kxkwp7qmr30n0twfkh9jb7y0
+  text: |-
+    Root cause: ProcessLanguageServerConnection.close() (Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/LSP/ProcessLanguageServerConnection.swift) sends SIGKILL to the child and returns immediately, without waiting for the kernel/Foundation to actually finish reaping the process. A caller checking `kill(pid, 0) == -1 && errno == ESRCH` immediately after close() returns can race that asynchronous reap.
+
+    Tried and reverted: added `process.waitUntilExit()` (Foundation's blocking Process.waitUntilExit()) inside close() right after kill(SIGKILL), to force a deterministic wait. This DEADLOCKED — confirmed via `sample` on the hung `swift test` process, stack stuck inside `-[NSConcreteTask waitUntilExit]` called from close(). Reverted; close() is back to its original form.
+
+    Fix landed instead in the test only (Tests/FoundationModelsCodeContextTests/ConnectionTests.swift):
+    - Added private static `pidExists(_ pid: Int32) -> Bool` helper (`kill(pid, 0) == 0 || errno != ESRCH`).
+    - In aRequestTimeoutThatPropagatesUncaughtStillClosesTheConnectionAndReapsTheProcess(), replaced the single immediate `#expect(kill(capturedPID, 0) == -1 && errno == ESRCH)` with a bounded poll (100 x 10ms = 1s budget) polling pidExists, matching the existing sibling pattern in waitUntilExitReturnsOnceTheScriptRunsOut() (which polls connection.isRunning() for the analogous Process.isRunning-lags-real-state race).
+
+    Verification: swift build clean; swift test --filter ConnectionTests looped 25 consecutive times, 20/20 passing every run, zero failures, no hangs; full swift test suite (485 tests, 41 suites) passed once. Adversarial double-check dispatched to review before handoff.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-15T21:59:52.820963+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: Investigate flaky ConnectionTests.aRequestTimeoutThatPropagatesUncaughtStillClosesTheConnectionAndReapsTheProcess

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -30,10 +30,18 @@

 The assertion checks that a killed/reaped subprocess's PID no longer exists (`kill(pid, 0)` returns -1 with `ESRCH`). This is inherently a race against the OS actually finishing reaping the process; under load or scheduler jitter the check can run before the process is fully gone.

+## Root cause
+`ProcessLanguageServerConnection.close()` sends `SIGKILL` to the child and returns immediately, without waiting for the kernel/Foundation to actually finish reaping the process. A caller checking `kill(pid, 0) == -1 && errno == ESRCH` immediately after `close()` returns can race that asynchronous reap.
+
+An attempted production fix (`process.waitUntilExit()` inside `close()` right after the `kill(SIGKILL)`) was tried and reverted: it deadlocked (confirmed via `sample` on the hung process, stuck in `-[NSConcreteTask waitUntilExit]` called from `close()`). `close()` is unchanged from its original form.
+
+## Fix
+Test-only fix in `ConnectionTests.swift`: added a `pidExists(_:)` helper and replaced the single immediate assertion with a bounded poll (100 x 10ms = 1s budget), mirroring the existing sibling pattern in `waitUntilExitReturnsOnceTheScriptRunsOut()`.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Root-cause why the reap-completion check can observe the PID as still present
-- [ ] Either add a bounded retry/poll (e.g. poll `kill(pid, 0)` for a short timeout instead of asserting once immediately after the close call) or otherwise make the assertion deterministic
-- [ ] Loop `swift test --filter ConnectionTests` at least 20x locally with no failures before closing
+- [x] Root-cause why the reap-completion check can observe the PID as still present
+- [x] Either add a bounded retry/poll (e.g. poll `kill(pid, 0)` for a short timeout instead of asserting once immediately after the close call) or otherwise make the assertion deterministic
+- [x] Loop `swift test --filter ConnectionTests` at least 20x locally with no failures before closing

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `swift test --filter ConnectionTests` passes reliably across repeated runs
\ No newline at end of file
